adds.rationale: BOTZ fills the robotics wave — named in the profile as a likely next wave and the only declared wave with zero watchlist representation — as the sector transitions from science-project to buildout, per Morgan Stanley's April 2025 $5T long-run forecast and a ~39% CAGR projected through 2030.

news_evidence.summary: Morgan Stanley's 'A $5 Trillion Global Market' report projects ~1B humanoid units by 2050, with labor-cost substitution across physical industries as the structural driver — the mechanism in the profile's robotics thesis.
MarketsandMarkets sizes humanoid robots at $2.92B in 2025 growing to $15.26B by 2030 (39.2% CAGR) on falling AI compute costs, reshoring, and aging-population automation — buildup-phase entry timing.

removes.rationale: STNG's rerouting-premium thesis has breached: Q2 2025 net income fell 68% YoY to $73.5M and average daily TCE dropped from $38,813 (2024) to $25,569, and management's September 4 filing locks a vessel into a 5-year charter at $28,350/day rather than riding spot — a classic 'exit before the crest' signal.

news_evidence.summary: Scorpio Tankers Q2 2025: net income $73.5M vs $227.3M YoY (-68%); average TCE fell to $25,569 from $38,813; dividend cut to $0.40/share — sustained freight-rate normalization.

Scorpio 6-K (Sep 4, 2025): locks the LR2 STI Orchard into a 5-year charter at $28,350/day while Q3 LR2 spot TCE of $32,700 remains ~16% below the 2024 peak — a signal spot rates are near/past their ceiling.

rationale_overall: Sole swap — STNG out, BOTZ in — tightens alignment with the profile's wave map. STNG exits on Q2-Q3 2025 fundamentals: freight rates 16-34% below 2024 peaks and management de-risking into long-term charters, signaling the rerouting premium has crested (the profile's guidance is to trim tankers as rates normalize). BOTZ closes the robotics gap — the only named tech wave with zero representation — as the April 2025 Morgan Stanley $5T forecast plus an accelerating ETF-launch cycle mark the transition from quiet buildup to early surge. Post-change spans AI (MSFT, NVDA), rockets (RKLB), quantum (QTUM), nuclear (NUKZ, CEG), defense (ITA), robotics (BOTZ), with defense the sole geopolitical expression after STNG's exit.
